subject: First Aid & Safety
title: How Sweating Cools You
description: A quick win: understand How Sweating Cools You.

hook.text: You have 2-4 million sweat glands. On a hot day, they produce up to 1.5 liters of sweat per hour.

buildup.text: Sweat itself doesn't cool you—its evaporation from skin absorbs heat energy and carries it away.

discovery.text: This is why humid heat feels worse—sweat can't evaporate when the air is already saturated.

twist.text: At 100% humidity and 95°F, your body literally cannot cool itself. Core temperature rises inevitably.

climax.text: Wet-bulb temperature—combining heat and humidity—is the true measure of heat danger.

punchline.text: It's not the heat. It's the humidity. When sweat can't dry, you can't cool.

quiz.question: Why does humid heat feel worse than dry heat?
options: Sweat can't evaporate when air is saturated
Water in the air conducts heat faster
